diagnoseV2.cancelMessage: You'll lose all the information you've entered.
diagnoseV2.freeTextPlaceholderBeginner: Describe what's happening in your own words — for example: 'My bike makes a clicking sound when I turn right'
diagnoseV2.freeTextPlaceholderAdvanced: Describe the issue — include RPM range, operating temperature, recent maintenance, specific components

subtitle.noise: Unusual sounds like clicking, grinding, rattling, or squealing
subtitle.vibration: Unusual shaking you can feel in the handlebars, seat, or pegs
subtitle.leak: Fluid dripping or pooling under the bike — oil, coolant, or fuel
subtitle.smoke: Visible smoke or steam from the exhaust, engine, or other areas
subtitle.warningLight: A warning indicator lit up on your dashboard
subtitle.performanceIssue: The bike feels less powerful, sluggish, or doesn't respond normally
subtitle.visualDamage: Visible cracks, dents, rust, or wear you can see
subtitle.smell: Unusual burning, fuel, or chemical odors
subtitle.engine: The main motor that powers the bike
subtitle.brakes: Front or rear brake pads, rotors, calipers, or lines
subtitle.exhaust: The pipes and muffler that carry exhaust gases away
subtitle.tires: The rubber tires, wheels, or rims
subtitle.electrical: Battery, lights, starter, wiring, or electronics
subtitle.suspension: The forks, shocks, or how the bike handles bumps
subtitle.chainDrivetrain: The chain, sprockets, belt, or shaft that drives the rear wheel
subtitle.bodywork: Fairings, fenders, tank, seat, or frame
subtitle.always: The issue is constant, no matter what you're doing
subtitle.atSpeed: Only when riding at higher speeds
subtitle.idle: When the engine is running but the bike isn't moving
subtitle.coldStart: When you first start the bike after it's been sitting
subtitle.hotEngine: After riding for a while when the engine is warm
subtitle.braking: When you apply the front or rear brake
subtitle.acceleration: When you twist the throttle to speed up
subtitle.turning: When leaning or turning the handlebars
subtitle.clicking: A rhythmic tick or click, often from the engine or drivetrain
subtitle.backfiring: Loud pops or bangs from the exhaust
subtitle.squealing: A high-pitched screech, often from brakes or belts
subtitle.wobble: The handlebars or frame shaking side to side
subtitle.hardToSteer: The bike feels heavy or resists turning
subtitle.spongyBrakes: The brake lever feels soft or goes too far before engaging
subtitle.rustCorrosion: Orange-brown buildup or pitting on metal surfaces
subtitle.discoloration: Parts changing color from heat, fluid, or chemical damage
subtitle.wontStart: The engine won't turn over or fire up
subtitle.stalling: The engine dies unexpectedly while running
subtitle.poorFuelEconomy: Using more fuel than usual for the same riding
subtitle.overheating: Temperature gauge reads high or you see steam
